step_by_step_thinking:
In patients with porcelain-fused-to-metal crowns (PFM), thermal tests like heat or cold can be less reliable due to the insulating properties of the alloy. Electric pulp testing (EPT) provides more accurate results as it bypasses surface conductance issues associated with PFM restorations. C D
My Answer: C The electric pulp test (EPT) is the best option for assessing pulpal sensation in patients with porcelain-fused-to-metal crowns because it measures direct electrical conductivity from tooth structure rather than relying on conduction through the restoration material. A B CD My Answer: C

The electric pulp test (EPT) is optimal for this scenario given that PFMs can interfere with traditional temperature-based tests by providing an insulated barrier between the probe and the pulp. Thus, I select option C. A B C D My Answer: C A B C D My
